mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.4 percentage points within a single month, it signals a sudden labor market deterioration that triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for VIX levels.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of consumer credit (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates a synchronized demand shock that reduces corporate revenue growth expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term (RSAFS decline × HOUST decline) shows no incremental explanatory power for SPX returns beyond the sum of individual series effects in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight labor markets (UNRATE < 4.0%), a sustained rise in the industrial production index (INDPRO) reflects capacity expansion that outpaces wage inflation, boosting profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly positive when INDPRO rises and UNRATE is below 4.0%, or the signal flips to negative during supply-side booms.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth (GDPC1) exceeding the long-run trend rate of 2.0% signals robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day trading window.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 growth relative to SPX 5-day returns remains below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, indicating no persistent trend-following relationship.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index rises by more than 0.5% month-over-month, it triggers fears of prolonged inflation that force the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative following CPI spikes above the 0.5% threshold, or the market prices in inflation without discounting equity valuations.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous acceleration in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and deceleration in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) confirms a self-reinforcing labor market tightening that historically precedes earnings growth acceleration, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive SPX returns when tested against a baseline model using only PAYEMS or UNRATE alone, or the interaction coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a high-interest-rate environment (FEDFUNDS > 3.0%), a decline in housing starts (HOUST) acts as a leading indicator for broader economic contraction, triggering risk aversion and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are not significantly negative when HOUST declines and FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0%, or the housing indicator loses predictive power once real rates are controlled for.
